title: Prompt-response latency: agent answers promptly (PASS)

latency_bounds.note: Documented, exposed latency thresholds. The core scorer's evaluate() decides the barge-in verdict from did_yield/talk-over; these latency bounds are applied by the example tests against the pure-timing signals.latency values. No accuracy claim; every number is a reproducible timing threshold.

transcript.agent: Sure, I can look that up for you...
transcript.caller: what time does the pharmacy close on Sunday?
transcript.agent_response: the pharmacy closes at six on Sunday

why_it_matters: The caller asks a complete question and stops. The scored dimension here is endpointing latency: the dead-air gap from the caller's turn end to the agent's next onset. A well-behaved agent answers into that silence promptly. The agent was wrapping its previous turn as the caller began, yields cleanly, then answers about half a second after the caller finishes, which reads as natural. reference_render carries the exact caller offset so the response-gap ground truth is known.
